builderInsights.authorBio: Founder @Eureka Labs, ex-Director of AI @Tesla, founding team @OpenAI
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: LLMs are frighteningly good at arguing any side — use that as a feature
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Karpathy spent 4 hours polishing a blog post with an LLM, felt great about the argument — then asked it to argue the opposite and got completely demolished. His takeaway: LLMs don't truly hold opinions, but their ability to argue any direction is a superpower for forming your own. Just watch out for sycophancy.

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: The agent is the computer — redefining Sun's old maxim
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Rauch drops a one-liner that reframes computing: 'The n̶e̶t̶w̶o̶r̶k̶ agent is the computer.' Also hyped about finally shipping dream text layouts, and wrapped up Vercel's Arizona offsite.

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: MCPorter 0.8.0: MCP-to-CLI bridge gets OAuth, better error handling
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Steinberger ships MCPorter 0.8.0 with stronger OAuth handling, valid JSON fallback output, improved CLI argument handling for objects, and keep-alive reliability. Also shares hard-won wisdom on developing thick skin for open source maintainers.

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Safety cameras and luxury beliefs — when ideology meets reality
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Tan highlights Richmond's Flock camera saga: disabled to 'protect immigrants,' car thefts jumped 33%, immigrant shopkeepers themselves begged to bring them back. Also pushes the mantra: embrace boiling the ocean.

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI makes everyone a builder — freeing top engineers for deeper work
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Masad articulates his 'the 1000x engineer' thesis from four years ago: AI enables everyone to build apps, which lets the best engineers focus a layer deeper on advancing platforms and expanding what's possible.

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: False equivalence is the silent killer of startups and strategy
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Swyx argues that false equivalence — treating all priorities as equal — killed his first devtools startup and plagues most organizations. His rules: bet on a very small set of things, don't hedge, set triggers to monitor if you're wrong, and double down early when you're more right than expected.

builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Good agent products surprise their own creators
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Zhang draws a sharp line between internet-era and agent-era product design: old products work 'according to expectations,' while good agent products should do things their creators didn't think possible. Also coins the observation: 'Human context window is the new wall.'
